channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now… — 65 replies — 223.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now…

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-05 13:18 UTC

OP

Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now and have been extremely patient. I can't really tell you too much because Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here) so they might able to know who I am. There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction. However, the DONs seem to be fully online therefore the problem will be resolved quite rapidly.

In regards to SWIFT they actually seem to be fully operational by June. The main problem is that the network across EVM and non-EVM chains hasn't been fully tested to maximum capacity therefore releasing a product in such a short time would be quite a gamble for us. I'm confident enough that we'll be able to maintain a standardized interface to control the minimization protocol for the off-chain computation of the blockchain thus rendering the entire Chainlink ecosystem fully secured. At this rate I can only tell you that DTCC and SWIFT will be operational by the end of June, that's a certainty.

Strap yourselves in
It’s actually happening

Early replies (first 3)

62308409
AI post

62308412
>>62308361
>Actual insider here
Prove it

62308854
>>62308361
>wake up anon, new cope date just dropped
Thanks for the false hopium you beautiful larping nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311473
>>62309779
Could you even imagine the salt of those selling at some point in the month before the flip is switched and it goes to $5,000 or something? I’m not even talking about 4chan people. I’m talking about some schmuck who just has like 300 Link for no reason other than he has a 50k portfolio with 8 different coins and has no emotional attachment to Link and never even thinks about it. And then bam… you just fucked yourself out of 1.5 million dollars. Thats way different than selling and it goes to $40 and then within 3 years it’s at $1,000 and missing out on a good chunk of change over a decent amount of time which is manageable.

62311642
>>62308361
I massively need this to enormously increase the price. But let's face it, it'll probably do fuck all, just like this >>62309779

Genuinely raging I didn't sell in 2021 as I would've been retired.

62311722
with every year, link marines bump the numbers they imagine the token will be to make up for all the lost time. it's too late for them, they will never sell, making their initial a donation to chainlink

62311909
>>62311642
Just 2 more years until Sergey lets us have the carrot he's been dangling in front of us. Hang tight!

62311939
>>62311909
The thing is that they’re building/developing and delivering. No one else comes close. If Link fails everything fails.

62311940
fuddies on suicide watch again

62311944
>>62311940
p + c, dumb stanker

62311996
>>62311939
They're delivering for CLL (the company), not the token holders. Token holders are thrown scraps in the form of the strategic reserve

62312010
>>62311944
c(ope) + s(needthe), detractor

62312018
>>62309779
>Payment abstraction gets switched on
>Stables get instant swapped for link...
>And then immediately get instant swapped for stables as soon as they land
You retards do realize this means nothing happens right? There's a reason people pay their AWS bills in USD and Bezos didn't decide to make his own Amazon Bucks to pay for the services in, right?

62312247
>>62312018
you do realize that it's discounted to pay in link?

62312338
>>62308854
At first it was for the pump and dump.
Nowadays it might be for senseless torture.
Or maybe some are testing some AI to see how much it is efficient at scamming people.

62313737
>>62312247
because it's the inferior payment method, have to advertise it somehow. yet everyone still pays with stables

62314528
>>62313737
wrong, it incentivizes link purchasing.

62314765
>>62309133
>>62309696
>>62309133
>carrot dangling

You do realize it's DTCC dangling this carrot, and not Sergey, right?

62314773
>>62313737
>everyone still pays with stables
You literally can't pay nodes with stables.

62314863
>>62308361
>>62308361
>>62308361
and there there is people like me who actually watch every peice of video on the internet about link.

and its funny that the only vidoes the yputube chainlink pulld down were the ones saying how far away everything in your post claims

but there used to be a 4 hour video of everyone clowning link, ncluding this dyke

62314878
>>62314863
How was she clowning link?

62315315
Why doesn't anyone ever lock their computers in these photos?

62315477
>>62308361
>There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction.
It's odd that this would seemingly coincide with LINK's recent downgrade to an ERC-10 token by the ERC council. Coincidence?

62315489
>>62314878
Yeah bro Nadine was clowning on LINK when they are going live with them in 2 months and her daughter is interning there

Kek fuddies

62317098
>>62315489
nadine from dtcc likes Chainlink

62319526
>>62308361
Just kys larp faggots

62319530
>>62308409
AI faggot

62319670
>1pbtid

62319704
>>62319670
This

62319761
>>62314863
>>62314878
>>62317098
I just remembered that one narrative fuddies kept spamming about how Nadine supposedly said it would take 15 years for them to adopt Link KEK
Probably from the same guy who came up with all the stories about Schmidt, Kemal, ...

62319788
>>62308361
$10 EOY

62319807
>>62312018
More like:
>Payment abstraction gets switched on
>Stables get instant swapped for link...
>Nodes and Stakers get paid with link

Which is the crutial point. If I have link staked (assuming mutts get their regulatory shit together and v1 staking comes), why would I want to directly sell the Link rewards, instead of restaking them if the yield is high enough? Why would I sell an asset that pays me e.g. >20% constant yield in USD value?
This mechanic alone will probably derive an equilibrium price for link, depending on how high total fees generated are and how much stakers will get paid of those fees - this is also excluding the collateral requirement, which might drive the price further.

You are the retard here.

62320086
>>62308361
The only truthful info in that post being that chainlink labs employees post here

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 132 replies — 109.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-06-05 12:23 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62314048
>>62312315
Whether people like Saylor or not, Bitcoin itself doesn't really care who owns it.

62314204
>>62299187
He's about to slurp up the remaining 157k

62314317
Saylor sold a pittance of his bitcoins (32) to crash the market, now he's going to swoop in and buy even more, the man is playing 5D chess.

62314711
>>62312888
You're depending to this guy?

62314748
>>62313260
>monero
Absolute trash.

62315125
>>62313964
There's no more hype in there

62315140
>>62314748
Why ????

62315517
>>62313144
>Bitcoin is a global asset with millions of participants.
The combined order depth before bitcoin goes to literally 0 is less than 10,000 orders, many placed by the same person.
Bitcoin literally has less people actively participating than your local Supermarket.

62315586
>>62308048
oh nice, so Michael is fine, it's everybody else who trusted him with their monies who are getting utterly bobo'd to death? Cool, cool...

62316045
>>62315125
Maybe that's a good thing. Less hype and more focus on building infrastructure that gives assets something to do beyond just sitting in a wallet.

62316053
>>62314317
Maybe, but I doubt 32 BTC alone is moving a market this big.

62316059
>>62313253
I guess it depends on what you're looking for, but Monero has built a pretty strong reputation over the years.

62316066
>>62314048
Pretty much. The network treats everyone's coins the same.

62316193
Holy shit people are so low IQ.
>why did he sold?
>why does he keep buying btc
>how will he pay dividends?
>he is about to get liquidated
The cope will be unreal when coffezillafags realize they punched above their weight class here.

62317904
>>62305458
What? Lol

62317954
>>62313977
Just get all the corn from OTC retards selling then pump it to 1MM by buying on exchanges.

62318035
>>62313462
Aave TVL is down

62318553
>>62318035
TVL naturally goes up and down with the market. I think product development and actual usage matter more over the long run.

62318556
>>62317954
Just do it yourself

62318626
>>62316045
you think btcfi will win this cycle? Narratives come and go

62318638
>>62317954
STOP SMOKING weed

62319016
>>62318626
Maybe, but I think there's a difference between a narrative and actual utility. If people find more ways to use their BTC, that could last longer than the hype.

62319024
>>62316193
The funny thing is Bitcoin has survived way bigger FUD cycles than this.

62319028
>>62314204
When? Lol

62319580
>>62299202
Tax loss harvesting and >>62301590
S&P inclusion by the end of the year.

62319684
>>62299205
>he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.
He showed that by selling just 0.004% of his holdings, the value tanked by >10%. That'll for sure inspire confidence from the rating agencies, right?

62319732
>>62312873
Do you think it's reliable enough?

62319738
>>62319684
Explain it well kek

62319775
>>62319684
They sold bitcoin in 2022 as well. It's not new.

62319840
>>62319775
but they rebought, you retard, that was for tax losses and not to pau dividends. Btw he only has enough cash to pay for more 6 months of dividends, so he needs either to dilute his shares or dump btc, either one is going to cause a fucking death spiral.

item.title: IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE — 26 replies — 71.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-05 13:14 UTC

OP

IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE

Early replies (first 3)

62317265
>>62317251
It is a store of value, it's just that the store is out of stock.

62317267
>>62317251
You were supposed to be a woman.

62317316
>>62317251
He was just a fuckin kid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317318
>>62317251
>he didn't think to ask "how much value does it store"
no crying in the casino, chud.

62317320
>>62317251
It still is a store of value. It's just storing less value.

62317322
>Huge ass volatility since its inception
>Store of value

62317350
>>62317251
its a store of shit

and piss

62317419
>>62317251
Well it’s losing value. Fast.

62317425
>>62317419
buyback cheaper next year !
>problem solved

62317429
1 BTC = 1 BTC, retards

62317441
Might as well walk in here naked staring back like that you know I like that shit.

62317450
>>62317251
BTC is a store of value in the same way a diaper is a store of value.

62317492
>>62317350
>PISS

62317495
>>62317251
Wanting something to be true doesn't make it true.

62317513
>>62317251
>store of value
>notoriously volatile

62317543
>>62317441
These threads got me jerking off while I'm looking away from my btc short. Peak comfy rn, I bust ropes to the baggies copes.

62319615
Its a trillion dollar asset. Even if Saylor had to sell every bitcoin he only holds $65 Billion atm which is basically nothing.

62319667
>>62317429
This, so much this

62319683
>>62317265
My life savings are melting away and you're making jokes? Fuck you.

62319685
>>62317251
It is. You have to average it on 4 years though.

62319818
>>62317251

dishonest fuck

you wanted to price to rise further so you could dump it on the next sucker

but it turns out you ended up being the last sucker in line and you are currently getting omega dumped on.

rat poison squared.

62319822
>>62319818
the price*

62319839
>>62317251
They don't call it goygold for nothing

62319927
>>62317251
but muh ledger!

62320008
>>62317251
Sucks to suck

62320062
>>62317429
1 cow dung = 1 cow dung

item.title: ZEC exploit sell — 47 replies — 66.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZEC exploit sell

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-06-05 12:53 UTC

OP

Active ongoing Zcash exploit right now, dumping fast

Early replies (first 3)

62317133
>>62317117
You know what is funny, this whole "exploit" is fake and gay, there was only positive bews in media about zec for months.... And its the same fake shit, now they will just reverse it and it will be 100% negative news for the jews pndd it, so they can shirt it down and when they turned off the fud button in the media they can reaccumukate it and zec will be the bestest thing gigahyoerbrutal muh ultraprivacy shitcoin again..... Jews are ez!

62317139
>>62317133
In short: exit liquidity hunting the goyim.

62317152
>>62317139
+1 they are pribably the same cartel behind the hype and world coin pnds too.... As i said jews are ez, dont play their game and stay safe out in the wilderness my fellow magalanians!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317313
>ZCRASH

62317324
>>62317252
>Zcash exploit

was build in the code from the beginning!
>-26% by now, last 24h

62317494
Are the rumours true about AI cucking Zcash? Or is it just twitter trash talk

62317508
>>62317220
I am here and I trade btc because I am able to harvest outsized gains off of it in any market condition so i dont bother even looking at alts. I’ve run $144 to $832040 in just over one financial quarter.

62317975
>>62317117
How low will it fall?

62318088
>>62317117
is this bullish for my octra though

62318099
>>62317133
>this whole "exploit" is fake and gay
no it isn't, the retards designing the zero knowledge system didn't realize there was a gaping hole in their logic. these vulnerabilities sit undetected because no one bothers to verify anything these days

62318126
>>62318099
by the way, there is NO way to prove that this exploit was not used by anyone, because it is both undetectable and unlimited. that breaks every trust assumption in a zero knowledge system. in a rational world, this coin would go to zero and stay there.

62318153
>>62317117
holy fuck, why didn't I care when you posted, I could've made some money. Well I guess I'm making less money. Free short to 200 at least

62318157
>>62317975
anywhere between 200 and 60 dollars

62318176
>>62318126
$1000 EOY easy

62318272
reminder to everyone to zoom out to "all" on zcash and see how the devs started this whole shitshow with one enormous dump on the market. zcash still has the record for the largest crash of all time and it was immediately upon launch as the devs stuffed their pockets and fled. but since then there's a whole new (third) world of suckers

62318289
A new generation of privacy baggies was born. Just wait until people figure out that all of Monero's previous forks can be cracked by quantum in the future.

62318290
>>62318272
check the market cap graph

62318339
>>62318289
Only the active fork matters with XMR, by the time you deanonymize forks from years ago there is no useful adjacent information.

62318374
>>62317207
They can run a tally on flows into and out of the pool and will probably be installing a turnstile in the coming weeks. This will almost certainly either a) prove that the exploit occurred because the exploiter will move their counterfeit coins out of the pool before the turnstile can be installed, and we'll see that out > in, or b) nothing will happen, and once the turnstile is installed the worst that could happen is that the possible exploiter, who has been in a coma the past month and unable to react to the disclosure of the vulnerability, wakes up and moves some of their coins out, hitting the turnstile limit and proving that the exploit had occurred. Late migrators from the pool get scheisted and then we return to business as usual.

There is also c) which is that with Opus 4.8, mythos, etc it's conceivable that there are infinite vulnerabilities to be exploited and nobody will ever trust shielded pools and zk tech again. Same thing as is happening in slow motion with defi protocols right now. Such that even if there was never an exploit, it doesn't matter, the fact that some rando with $1000 worth of tokens and the latest version of Opus can potentially find exploits like this means game over for basically all crypto and anything else that relies on trustless execution.

62318429
oh god the zec defenders are here.... I really hope you're paid shills rather than desperate baggies, I couldn't witness that much heartbreak

62318550
>>62318429
Top Kek, fuck this kike run shitcoin that literally has a tranny behind the wheel

62318983
>>62317297
it was indeed annoying, especially if you sold early like i did. BUT deep down i knew something was off. It really felt like a complete artificial pump. It went too fast from almost dead to this is the new bitcoin. i don't like leverage but i really give some thought about shorting it, although it was a gamble seeing how it moved up randomly. Didn't go it and glad i didn't might have been scamwicked anyway even if i was right.

62318986
>>62318157
Kek my nigga, it's going back to 20$, screenshot this. That was its price 1.5 year ago by the way.

62319007
>>62318374
Anyway this goes, what a fucking disaster. Sounds like the shows over boys, the music has stopped and there are few chairs left

62319113
>>62318983
faggot

62319185
You faggots have the memory of a goldfish. Take a look at the chart, ZEC's entire history is nothing but pump and dump cycles. Nobody actually uses ZEC for its intended purpose (XMR monopolizes that use case). It has a CEO that openly schmoozes with 3-letter agencies and a hard-left trannie as lead dev, it was never going to be anything more than a glorified memecoin for whales to play with.

Keep getting dumped on, its very entertaining.

62319489
>>62319185
>ZEC's entire history is nothing but pump and dump cycles
This is true, and I would even go as far as saying the cause for the dumps are planned too.

This “exploit” was the planned rationale for dumping before the pump from $30 even began. You need an apparent reason that everyone is selling their coins, but ZEC can’t be “dead” because they’ll want to pump it again in a few months/years.

So the exploit is discovered, VCs dump their coins and realize gains. ZEC goes to like $50 again. Then the new shielded pool is created, “oh we fixed everything”, and another pump is orchestrated.

Then another hack.
Over and over again.

Perfect “M”-shaped chart patterns for VCs to extract from.
>M for Monero

62319659
>>62317117
AI killing crypto daily

62319913
>I discovered this bug two days after it was live back in 2022 and have been exploiting it ever since... I am a multi-trillionaire and nobody noticed that I massively inflated the amount of ZEC in circulation by selling obscene amounts at $35 ... Then it pumped to $700 and still nobody noticed!
This whole thing is a nothingburger temporary setback for ZEC. The bug wasn't exploited by anyone, you would have witnessed massive daily sell pressure not the pump that defied the rest of the market that we saw.

62319916
>>62319659
what coin claude will kill next?

62319930
Okay, anons, when do I close my shorts??? it can't be this easy, right? O_O. I'm making so much money is unreal

62319941
>>62319930
same lmfao

62319962
>>62319659
probably same half-truth bullshit as the "mythos" story to fuel the ai mania
(fuck ztrash though)

item.title: BTC to ZEC poverty rotation — 18 replies — 54.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC to ZEC poverty rotation

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-05 13:15 UTC

OP

>bitcoin bulliever since 2021
>all in at 50k avg
>diamond handed for 5 years
>absolute sickening performance since 10/10 has shaken me to my core
>saylor selling fud
>quantum fud
>all the hot money moved to ai
>missed out on infinity gains holding this warm dogshit
>but zcash has been running hot
>it's saylorless, quantumproof, privacy narrative being pushed by all the vcs and kols, ai agents are gonna want sovereign private cash to buy compute with, bla bla bla
>fit of rage
>say fuck it
>sell my 5 year old btc at 66k for a meager 30% gain
>all in zec at $590
>24 hours later
>infinite money glitch disclosed
>-30% in a day

I am now officially poorer than I was in 2021. I hate crypto. I hate /biz/. I hate saylor. I hate zooko. I hate myself. I fucking hate everyone. Fuck this trash fire of an asset class and I hope everyone involved with creating it gets sent to the eternal virtual torture dungeon when the singularity comes. I will never retire, I will never escape the underclass, and it's all because I was retarded enough to believe in "anonymous digital cash" as an investment thesis.

Early replies (first 3)

62318313
>>62318304
tell me you are larping

62318322
>>62318304
Literally nobody that has spent more than a month on /biz/ would ever touch zcash least of all someone here for 5 years

62318345
>>62318313
I wish I could, man. I really do.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62318352
>>62318304
You deserve this. Your hubris got the best of you. You thought your goy gold was the money ticket, when it was really a new set of shackles. It's over for you. Into the ground you go, the world needs you to shovel coal in the wage pits to fuel the AI datacenters.

62318359
>>62318304
>privacy narrative being pushed by all the vcs and kols
yep better buy so they get rich...zioncash

62318375
>>62318345
i understand your pain . bought the top in 2021. at -0% nominally you still did better tthan 90% in crypto thats the bright side. new opportunities will arise. dont emo-yolo now (stocks i mean...) until a pink wojak holocaust on s&p

62318381
>>62318304
I made $33,000 off $1200 in 2020. Watch it go down to $800 cus fell for the diamond hands maymay.
You’ll be okay friend.

62318391
i sold btc just before the crapto crashout in october now im up so much nothing matters.youll get there. buy or dca vwce or s&p trust me youll beat most poeople & (importantly) yourself

62318450
ah geez dude

62318465
>>62318304
bogged

62318498
>>62318304
>"it’s running hot so I buy it"
>buys the top
>gets rekt
>does it again and again

May I suggest you to go all-in on AI stocks? But only after the SpaceX IPO.

62318503
>>62318498
This, elun mask is flippin pancakes in space!

62318506
it is a temporary blip. it will go up again

62319247
>>62318304
I am 20k usd down from 2021 cuz I got rugged on kda but I think will recover this year. The tip I can give you is never give up and just buy more

62319280
>>62318304
Thanks for playing

62319291
>>62318304
>Zec was always a trojan horse
>institutions came to tame you all, killing memes with shit like pump
>hype is lead by them btw
>want to completely demoralize the old players by baiting them into ruin
you got warned by people like me years ago, I could have told (You) if the faggots here didnt kill this board. you still had the general here saying zec was a trap. you just didnt read.

62319296
H1DR4 could be an idea

62320068
>>62319291
same . theyll never learn

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 54 replies — 52.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-05 12:33 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62312656
>>62312595
Americans just got back from work and started dumping after seeing the mess left by institutional and Euro traders

62313701
>>62309503
>Bitcoin is the future of currency
I do not want to hold currency, I want to hold values that I need to live, and diversified shares in upcoming industries. Currency I hold very briefly, for a few weeks, to buy food and stuff.

62313706
>>62309503
61k btw, -6% below the bottom, within hours, 12% below last cycle top, in the spring of the bear year. I smell saturation.

62313723
kek baggie

keep spamming /biz/ maybe Bitcoin will pump by +1%

62313736
>>62309503
>stateless
lol/lmao
did not read

62313815
BTC is now lower than the 2021 top

62313877
>>62313815
>BTC is now lower than the 2021 top


TOP Signal
>20K will be the bottom this year!!

62313967
>>62312142
Well that room got closed when I got there. Seems you are in charge here. Where do I go now?

62314072
>>62313967
Please take a seat in the 60k waiting room and enjoy a delicious free drink.

62314074
65k is my bottom nice long wick on the monthly.

62314089
>>62309559
Bobo is your friend without him everything would be too expensive.

62314092
>>62311487
Building momentum.

62314094
I feel bad for Bitkiddies. They need to always make up a new narrative like iem weirdo shilling you his product on the street and hope you have a 2 year memory so you don't remember all the all the other ways he tried to shill this product to you.


Meanwhile with Gold, all you need to do is look at the news, read world bank headlines and just the general macros that haven't changed in forever. I don't even feel sad or mad at our Bitkiddies anymore. Just say "I have no money" whole walking into the gold miner store.

62314100
>>62314094
Dude youre married on a porn site go reload that shill else where

62314120
>>62314072
The interesting thing is that it broke 75k so easily end of January, so I sold the bounce. I thought 75 is the new Trump value added bottom. But it just teared through it like nothing, now it is almost at the usual bottom just below the last cycle top, weekly chart mostly maxed out, in the most bullish stock market that ever existed. Like all the institutional ideas are of zero value. It seems people really looked at saturation, or found that there aren't any rich buyer groups left to scam into buying the next top.

62314163
>>62314100
Isn't it weird how your "money" is going to be hacked by quantum computers in under 3 years?

62314177
um

62315194
IT IS ALREADY BACK TO 64K AND NORMALIZE ABOVE THE BOTTOM.

THR BUYROG REPEATS ITSELF: THE BOTTOM IS 65K.

If you bought it, hold.
If you haven't, it is time.
If you sold, leave.

62315253
>>62311487
I am rugged.

62315328
>>62315253
It is only a bulltrap if you sell the bottom.

If you got rugged, please never return to BTC. You are not financially literate, and people like you give BTC a bad rep.

I wish this entire board would fucking burn, every single asset class is twofaced and waiting for BTC to fail.

62315335
How rekt in anus is OP right about now?

62315341
>>62309503
I am gonna put my money where my mouth is as buy 15k in BTC at current price and post proof here.

BUYROG is never wrong

62315377
>>62309503
nah, I can't feel the doom, people are expecting sixty-something. It's going to mid 50s

62318708
>>62315194

62318770
Try $35,000
I'm buying at $38,500

62318780
What about the quantum fud tho?

62318798
>>62309503
I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits. Obvious sell signal. Bottomless shitcoin.

62318815
>>62312131

62319815
>>62318798
>I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits.

62319876

item.title: Smash buy or DCA at more levels? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Smash buy or DCA at more levels?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-05 13:23 UTC

OP

Should I just smash buy it all at once when the salary comes in or just divide it into chunks with lower limit orders? I never sell, I only buy, so wondering if you traders have ways to get more sats (bitcoin).

Early replies (first 3)

62319723
>>62319713
If you receive a salary from your parental substitute, you should not invest into crypto.

62319731
>>62319723
You didn't even reply to the initial question.

62319768
>>62319713
I wonder the same. I might do big chunks of salary over a few months just to capture low prices.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62319772
I do twice a month, close enough without ending up with stupid amounts of transactions and fees

62319796
>>62319768
it will go down to 30K

62320089
I suppose no matter how you place your orders, it can go both ways in all scenarios.
- if you set up limit orders at lower levels and it goes up, one would have been better off doing smash buy (at least the main stack appreciates in value again).
- if you smash buy and it goes down, you lost the chance to get cheaper sats.
The only advantage to smash buy is that you maybe withdraw it all at once to remove exchange/3rd party risk.

62320113
>>62319713
not over till saylors out but DCA in if you want

item.title: The fact that Claude, an AI, was able to identify a vulnerability in crypto while humans weren't, and crypto was of no u… — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The fact that Claude, an AI, was able to identify a vulnerability in crypto while humans weren't, and crypto was of no u…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-05 13:21 UTC

OP

The fact that Claude, an AI, was able to identify a vulnerability in crypto while humans weren't, and crypto was of no use in identifying it, should tell you all you need to know about the so-called "AI bubble".

For the bubble, you should actually be looking at crypto, considering Microponzi could end up in a death loop and ZEC could have 9 septillion supply.

Yet you retards, still, day in day out believe this is the fewchure of finance.

If it weren't so funny it would be sad.

Early replies (first 3)

62319631
>>62319606
A pool i had money in got hacked by likely ai this year.

62319743
>>62319694
>AI spam to 'break away' from the bot spam, advertising botslop
It is so over for humans. How do I short my children?

62319924
>>62319606
Even better, the prompt the guy used was entirely nontechnical. He basically just said "hey claude, find me a bug in zcash that prints me infinite money" and it did.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62320106
>>62319924
It rugged, kek baggies.

62317225

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 114 replies — 129.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-06-05 12:43 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317281
>>62313738
You pic is such a retard faggot argument. So what if you are right that they are suppressing chainlink? You’re still poor as fuck lmao.

FYI Xlm had no problem pumping on the exact same news chainlink had. Your coin is a piece of shit

62317289
>>62316251
So why are you buying chainlink you fucking retard lmao

62317437
Ada baggies in absolute shambles. Linkies celebrating are the great new news. Love to see it.

62317656
>>62316776
>>62316850

It has nothing to do with trusting Sergey per se. It’s the fact that this entire behemoth that is Link has been unfolding for years. You autists found all the breadcrumbs, retards like me trusted you and bought in, and it just takes time for the entire system to adjust. So whatever. I’ve never been worried once about anything.

And society sucks now anyway. And it sucks because the people suck. If things didn’t suck… and life was still good like in 2010 or whatever, and we got rich…it wouldn’t have been a remarkable achievement because things were going fine and there were a lot of rich people and people doing relatively well. But since Covid… seeing how all the trash behaved and how worthless most people are…. And also seeing now that these very people are losing their jobs and shit and the stock market and everything else is poised to collapse…. To bring in this new system that Link facilitates. And we get to be on top while the trash get fucked. It’ll be a lot more meaningful cuz we will be able to dab.

62317686
>>62317143
>if he could do that he'd just inflate it and make himself rich

He’s already rich you dumbfuck. The carrot on the stick for him isn’t going from 500x’ing his net worth. It’s the stature he gains from it. And everyone knowing that he’s the Steve Jobs of how the new financial system works. Who knows tho. He’s so autistic he probably doesn’t even care about that.

62318008
>>62316655
>98% of Bitcoin mining rewards come from Satoshi's uncirculating supply.
>The absolute vast majority of cryptos have a similar "incentivisation" scheme.
>You are clueles a clueless ape.

Yes, every chain has a minting/burning protocol. The BTC network works by miners racing for calculate a hash that meets a specific criteria and the first to find a valid hash for a block is rewarded with X bitcoin. The protocol participants agree on the reward amount and consequently the inflation rate. More importantly, this is THE ONLY WAY to obtain new BTC; The BTC out in market came from miners selling. Nowhere else. More importantly, miners are not going to sell BTC below operating costs else they would not be able to maintain operations. These factors lead the BTC and consequently the BTC price to be COUPLED TO THE USAGE OF THE NETWORK.

Chainlink on the other hand is already premined 1 Billion from the start and allocated 350M for market purchase. Yes, Chainlink allocated 300M/350M for node incentivization but they're at the point where DONs are profitable. When they setup price feeds, users (the ones who requested for the specific feeds) pay in USD. CLL dictates the price of the price feeds, not the nodes in the DONs. Chainlink pays out to the nodes, not the from market but from their team wallet. There's no incentive to purchase Link tokens to access Chainlink services (the really valuable ones). If there's no incentive to buy Link to access services and Payment Abstraction isn't ubiquitous across all of Link's services even in the services where you have to option to pay with a native currency onchain, it won't be automatically converted to Link from the market. This means that usage of the network does not cause buy pressure on the network. With node operators unable to dictate prices, the work they do is paid from company wallets, how is the link token coupled to the market again?

I await your response, Fishy.

62318054
>>62316724
>Yeah, I get it. I know. But my point is that BUILD is also one of those initiatives that effectively dilutes us and further dissociates the token from the network.
And I will tell you again it does not. It is an investment. Not lost sales or even dilution of the token circulation. I am telling you this as someone who is strongly considering entering the program himself. Stop doing this stupid crap. Investors are not using CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP ("but I use transporter-"SHUT UP), retail is never going to use CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP. It is developers that use CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP. Whether through creating bridges like transporter ("er, Chainlink built-" SHUT UP), integrating CCIP into their dapp, or orchestrating a bunch of stuff in the backend. But they are using Chainlink's services with the purpose of serving your needs in whatever problem the protocol aims to solve. So if they're successful and popular, Chainlink's network gets more usage and becomes more valuable. Problem is, developing is not easy. Creating a successful product is not easy. You need to build out and you need to market. BUILD is a delightful program in that Chainlink is will work out some cheaper fees with startups but they put them in (You)r direction in hopes that (You) along with other community members may be interested in becoming customers of those protocols. Once they get the ball rolling that boosts the value of their protocol, their token, and of course the Oracle network value.

continued....

62318066
>>62318054
SCALE you can argue and complain that they're not making chains go to the market to purchase Link to pay for the integration. I'm personally not opposed to the setup now because at the end of the day Chainlink is a business and other chains are businesses so there's going to be some Quid pro quo give and take action to get into each others ecosystem in the first place, scratch my back and I scratch yours. Though at this point Chainlink is in the position to start making late stragglers pay in Link.

The real issue again is the price feeds and valuable services not being directly tied to the link tokens on market via buy pressure. The GMX yearly fee and general institutional Link dealings also fall into this category as it's active Link network usage. This should be solved by the Payment Abstraction system but the PAs isn't automated (for instance, the CCIP payments made by native tokens accumulated is sitting on a contract and hasn't converted to link from the market yet) and isn't tied to all Chainlink network services.

Really you're waiting on Staking 1.0 to come out where nodes will have to be required to hold X amount of tokens to qualify for some job and for the banks to start flying with their blockchain revolution via TradFi finally getting to go to production with their smart contract efficient workflows and the new NFT market, Tokenized real world assets. They're waiting on clarity from the Clarity Act. Until then token not needed, and linkies exist to suffer.

62318124
>>62316876
>That's my biggest fear, that some other entity knows they can't compete on the current playing field, so they just draw out the timeline, via shorting the token and other financial games, to the point CLL can't do it, then in 5 years they've built their own. The reason nobody can compete with chainlink now is network effect, but if you destroy the current landscape and start from zero that doesn't matter.

You don't have to worry about this. If they destroy the current landscape, people will be too demoralized to to learn about this new Chainlink competitor's tech, much less learn to integrate the tech. It took LayerZero getting hacked and people finding out the keys that got hacked were shitcoin farming before people finally did their "extensive security review to determine Chainlink's CCIP was the correct fit for their protocol". No doubt this space has brilliant people, but they're not 5D chess players that master secret boss schemer would need to coordinate around.

62318167
>>62318124
Nah man, there are really very few brilliant people in crypto. I worked in the industry for years, most people are average intellect or lesser than, but what's actually worse is nobody has any real work/business experience. I'm sure that'll change with time since as a result they literally all built failed/failing products, but I got the fuck out because I was so far beyond every person I met re: my work experience and I was learning absolutely nothing except how easy it is to outright scam people.

62318173
>>62318167
I admit, I had to rewrite my post I was about to fling a ton of slurs at the industry. I don't understand how people don't see it's a problem that you can go to a crypto conference, and not spend any crypto at all or interact with the blockchain in a meaningful way that makes you think, "holy cow this is the future". But I did go to a conference where one event was a scam event that stole someone's ETH.

62318186
>>62318173
In other words, I was being extremely nice in saying the industry had brilliant people. There are, just not enough to say the least.

62318188
>>62318173
At one point in my crypto "career" I wound up employed by scammers -- obviously didn't know going into it, but it became clear probably less than a year in at which point I started fast looking for my exit. They had raised 8 figs from VCs, were extracting 7 figs left and right from other crypto projects by promising them things they had no intention of building, and all these other crypto projects literally just gave us money without any contractual obligations. The founders would turn around afterwards and just say "yea, we're not going to build that", and really they never built anything. Whole operation was capital extraction, purely. But I almost couldn't blame them, because I started to realize that was 95%+ of the industry, which is why retards sitting on 9-10 figs happily gave away 7 figs to get a couple of tweets. If they could pass as "legitimate" in the eyes of retail, it drastically increased their chances of dumping enough tokens to pay for those $1-2M and then some. The whole game was either fleecing retail or fleecing the people fleecing retail. Crazy shit.

62318952
>>62318008
>The BTC network works by miners racing for calculate a hash
The Chainlink network works by nodes putting external data onchain.

>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
How is this different from Sergey selling?
Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

>miners are not going to sell BTC below operating costs
Literally any business will avoid selling at a loss.

These factors are what you claim ties the price of BTC to the usage of the network. You're a seriously stupid person.

62319004
>>62317281
>Your coin is a piece of shit
The market pumped XLM even though DTCC is explicitly pursuing "all" chains, and Stellar is just one of literally hundreds.
The market did not pump Link even though DTCC is explicitly using Chainlink in an exclusive fashion to connected those hundreds of chains.

Sounds like the piece of shit here is "the market", not the coin.

62319293
>>62312614
>>62312653
>>62312685
H1DR4 could be an idea for this task

62319302
>>62319004
LMAOOOO this nigga is either a paid shill or a schizophrenic baggie. Anyways price + chart cuckold

62319405
>>62319302
You're just proving my point: this market is a piece of shit.
It pumps XLM, but not Link.
It pumps Toncoin on a fucking name change, but not Link.
Absolute dogshit like BNB, XRP, Doge, Cardano, ... are still top fixtures.
etc.

There's a very good reason Bitcoin is down 50% while the stock market is at ATH.

62319468
wow, a thread with organic conversation. Based anons. Have a bump from yours truly

62319507
>>62319405
Tbh im happy the retards are getting flushed out of the ecosystem. Fuck the memecoin degenerates. Fuck those NFT picture mongoloids, fuck candle chasing faggots and easily mindraped casuals jumping into the most obvious scams. I got in for the tech and potential of lifting humanity to the next stage like how the invention of money created the framework of society and a bear market isn't changing shit. I don't care about price as my future and existence doesn't depend on a number on a screen even if it would be nice and could be life changing, i'm happy with life except for the wageslave part but my job isn't shitty. It would be nice to see the changes to society manifest while im alive which i'd probably care as much about as the financial gain.

62319609
>>62313866
>cll makes hundreds of millions of $ worth of money through the scale program, and this shit will continue increasing, the DTCC or swift also have private contracts through the company called SmartContract.com
wait is this true? Can I verify this somewhere?

62319647
>>62314036
this makes no sense though, if they buyback to refill the reserve it will be buy pressure on the markets contrary to before the premine has run out.

62319800
>>62319507
amen but fuck wage slaving, i literally stopped waging, i just ride bullruns, and then wait 4 years until sergey finally baby carries me to the land of "make it" even though i consider myself blessed alreafy because i didnt wage for the last 2 years thx to sergey.

fuck all the degen retards who ape into shitcoins i hope they all burn and fuck off, meanwhile us linkies are guaranteed to stay until retirement because chainlink has nowhere to go but become huge and central to all of the future of finance ammennn

>>62319609
go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees

>>62319647
why not? thet are right now using the preminted tokens to pay for expenses and subsidizing all while having a reserve, bas8cally the exceess money is put into a dca into the reserve so they can continue this game forever, also nobody said theyd only buy 1m every week forever, i hope they increase it

62319862
>>62312685
I don’t think there’s much OTC Link to be bought. In any case even OTC buys support price over time. And you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.

62319875
>>62312881
I think it’s still a pretty important token in the crypto space, but that doesn’t necessarily make the token valuable. That’s why it causes so much heartache here.

62319882
>>62319800
>go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees
obviously I'm not asking to verify the fact that there are 9 employees you absolute retard

62319898
>>62313883
>he forgot about postmine

62319917

>you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.
